title: Dynamic Programming
analogy: DP is like filling out a multiplication table — instead of recalculating 7×8 every time, you look up the answer you already computed in the table.

overview.summary: Dynamic Programming solves complex problems by breaking them into overlapping subproblems, solving each once, and storing the result to avoid redundant computation.
learningObjectives: Identify overlapping subproblems and optimal substructure.
Implement both top-down (memoization) and bottom-up (tabulation) approaches.
Derive recurrence relations from problem constraints.
overview.interviewRelevance: DP is one of the most frequently tested advanced topics at FAANG companies, appearing in 20-30% of medium/hard interview problems.

paragraphs: Spell-checkers use DP (edit distance) to suggest corrections. GPS navigation uses DP to find shortest paths. Text diff tools (git diff) use DP (LCS) to show what changed.
Your phone's keyboard predictions use DP-based sequence models to predict the next word.
realWorldHook.ahaMoment: DP trades memory for time — by remembering past results, it transforms exponential brute-force into polynomial elegance.

definition.technicalDefinition: Dynamic Programming is a method for solving optimization problems by combining solutions to overlapping subproblems. It requires two properties: optimal substructure and overlapping subproblems.

vocabulary.definition: Top-down approach: solve recursively and cache results in a hash map.
Bottom-up approach: fill a table iteratively from the smallest subproblems.
A formula expressing the solution in terms of solutions to smaller instances.
The set of variables that uniquely define a subproblem.

keyProperties: Optimal substructure: an optimal solution contains optimal solutions to subproblems.
Overlapping subproblems: the same subproblems are solved multiple times.
Transforms exponential recursion into polynomial time by caching.

operations.name: Top-Down (Memoization)
operations.description: Solve recursively but cache results so each subproblem is computed only once.
operations.visualNote: Recursion tree with grayed-out branches for cached subproblems.

operations.name: Bottom-Up (Tabulation)
operations.description: Build a table from the base cases upward, filling each cell using the recurrence.
operations.spaceComplexity: O(n) or O(1) with space optimization

code.python:
def fib(n):
    if n <= 1: return n
    dp = [0] * (n + 1)
    dp[1] = 1
    for i in range(2, n + 1):
        dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2]
    return dp[n]

operations.visualNote: A 1D array fills left-to-right, each cell derived from previous cells.

practice.title: Coin Change
practice.keyInsight: dp[amount] = min(dp[amount - coin] + 1) for each coin.

practice.title: Longest Common Subsequence
practice.keyInsight: 2D table where dp[i][j] considers first i chars of s1 and first j chars of s2.

practice.title: 0/1 Knapsack
practice.keyInsight: Include or exclude each item, track remaining capacity.

practice.title: Edit Distance
practice.keyInsight: Three operations: insert, delete, replace — min of all three transitions.

conceptQuiz.question: What two properties must a problem have to be solvable by DP?
conceptQuiz.explanation: DP requires that optimal solutions contain optimal sub-solutions AND that subproblems recur.

conceptQuiz.question: What is the difference between memoization and tabulation?
options: Memoization is top-down recursive; tabulation is bottom-up iterative
conceptQuiz.explanation: Memoization starts from the original problem and caches. Tabulation builds from base cases upward.

recognitionQuiz.problemStatement: Find the minimum cost to reach the top of a staircase where each step has an associated cost, and you can climb 1 or 2 steps at a time.
recognitionQuiz.explanation: The problem has overlapping subproblems (ways to reach step i) and optimal substructure (min cost uses min of sub-costs).
